tests_run: cd client && npx playwright test (council.spec.js — chromium desktop 1440x900 + mobile 390x844)
risks: DOCUMENTED baseline regeneration: the 12 visual baselines (setup/answers/verdict × dark/light × desktop/mobile) were intentionally regenerated because the a11y fix darkened the light-theme verdict tokens (--verdict-supported/partial/refuted to 700/800-level) and raised --muted-foreground contrast — a deliberate, reviewed pixel change, not drift. Re-run after regeneration was stable (no diffs).

All 3 serious + 2 moderate a11y violations from the prior FAIL are resolved: aria-label added to the 3 shadcn Progress usages; verdict text tokens darkened to clear WCAG AA 4.5:1 on white + soft tints (verified via axe color-data probe); brand promoted to <h1>; session-progress strip given a landmark role. axe now reports 0 critical / 0 serious / 0 moderate.
The a11y scan now settles entrance animations before analyzing (transient framer-motion opacity is not a WCAG failure; WCAG assesses the stable state).
Deep flow (Round 2 / consensus / Round 3 verification) exercised via deterministic mocked SSE ($0); a real live end-to-end run earlier also passed with all 4 models + cross-vendor verification.

Mobile golden-path + mobile a11y are skipped by design (desktop-only specs; logic is viewport-independent, mobile still covered by visual baselines); claude-in-chrome was not connected so runtime diagnostics used Playwright headless.
